title: Conditionals: choose the correct verb form

items.text: If it {1} tomorrow, we will stay home.
items.explanation: First conditional: if + present simple, will + base verb. 'If it rains…'.

items.text: If I {1} rich, I would travel the world.
items.explanation: Second conditional uses the past form ('were' for all persons) for an unreal present: 'If I were rich…'.

items.text: She would call you if she {1} your number.
items.explanation: Second conditional: if + past simple. 'if she had your number'.
Zero conditional for general truths: present simple in both clauses. 'it melts'.

items.text: We {1} late if we don't hurry.
items.explanation: First conditional result clause: will + base verb. 'we will be late'.

items.text: If they had asked, I {1} helped them.
items.explanation: Third conditional: if + past perfect, would have + past participle. 'I would have helped'.
